title: Find the repeating and missing number

content.problemStatement:
You are given multiple test cases. Each test case consists of an integer array arrarrarr of size nnn, containing numbers in the range [1,n][1, n][1,n].
In each test case:


Exactly **one number appears twice** (the repeating number).
Exactly **one number is missing** from the range [1,n][1, n][1,n].
Your task is to return the result for each test case as an array of size 2:


The first element is the **repeating number**.
The second element is the **missing number**.
👉 **Important**: You are **NOT allowed to modify the original array**.

Function DeclarationFunction NamefindRepeatingAndMissingfindRepeatingAndMissingfindRepeatingAndMissing – This function finds the repeating and missing numbers in the given array without modifying it.

Parameters
arrarrarr : An integer array of size nnn containing numbers in the range [1,n][1, n][1,n].
Return Value

Returns an array of size `2`:



The **first element** is the repeating number.
The **second element** is the missing number.


Constraints
1≤T≤101 ≤ T ≤ 101≤T≤10
2≤n≤1052 ≤ n ≤ 10^52≤n≤105
n==arr.lengthn == arr.lengthn==arr.length
1≤arr[i]≤n1 ≤ arr[i] ≤ n1≤arr[i]≤n
Exactly one number appears twice.
Exactly one number is missing.
The original array must **not** be modified.

Input Format


The first line contains a single integer `T` — the number of test cases.




For each test case:



The first line contains a single integer `n` — the size of the array.
The second line contains `n` space-separated integers representing the array `arr`.




Output Format


For each test case, print two space-separated integers:



The repeating number
The missing number
constraints: Exactly one number appears twice and exactly one number is missing.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains an integer n, the size of the array. Second line contains n space‑separated integers arr[i] (1 ≤ arr[i] ≤ n). Exactly one number appears twice and exactly one number from 1..n is missing.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print two space‑separated integers on a new line: the repeating number followed by the missing number.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: array [4,1,2,2,5] → repeating 2, missing 3. Second test case: array [7,1,3,4,5,6,7] → repeating 7, missing 2.
Array [2,2,3] has 2 twice and 1 is absent.

solution.approach: We need to identify the element that occurs twice (repeating) and the element that does not occur at all (missing) in a permutation of 1..n where exactly one duplication and one omission exist. The simplest way without modifying the original array is to count frequencies. Create an auxiliary integer array freq of size n+1 initialized to zero. Scan the given array once and increment freq[value]. A second pass over 1..n reveals the index i with freq[i]==2 (repeating) and the index with freq[i]==0 (missing). This runs in O(n) time and O(n) extra space per test case, which easily satisfies the constraints. The algorithm is deterministic and works for all edge cases, including the smallest n=2 and the largest n=10^5.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) per test case, O(total N) for all test cases combined.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) auxiliary space per test case for the frequency array.
